title: Pleurospermum rupestre
taxonomy: Pleurospermum rupestre is classified within the family Apiaceae. This family also includes other plants commonly known as carrots, parsley, and dill. Pleurospermum rupestre can be further classified within the genus Pleurospermum, a group of flowering plants that are primarily native to New Zealand. The species is often found growing in rocky areas and is characterized by its small, white flowers. It is considered an endemic species, meaning it is found only in the wild in its native region.

intro: Pleurospermum rupestre, also known as Rock Carrot or New Zealand Rock Carrot, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its yellow flowers and edible leaves and stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Bioactive constituents of Pleurospermum rupestre include sesquiterpenes, alkaloids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support respiratory health and provide relief from symptoms of colds and flu.
